title: service-accounts — model relationships
description: ServiceAccount has TWO outbound FKs (application_id → applications, tenant_id → tenants) and ONE loose reference (signer_kid → auth::jwt_signing_keys.kid; not a schema-level FK because the auth module owns key lifecycle). There are NO inbound FKs from other modules — SAs are terminal in the ownership graph; nothing else references them.

outbound.description: Required. Binds the SA to exactly one Application. Immutable after creation. Restrict on delete so an Application soft-delete does not orphan SA rows; ops must delete the SAs first.

Optional (nullable). When populated, scopes the SA to a specific tenant. Cascade on delete: erasing the tenant hard-deletes the tenant-scoped SAs. Constraint enforced additionally by observer: tenant.application_id MUST match SA.application_id (APPLICATION_MISMATCH_ON_TENANT).

Reference by KID string, NOT by a UUID FK. The auth module owns kid lifecycle. The observer validates at write time (signer_kid must exist + be enabled + be scoped to the SA's application_id when the kid has application scoping). Disabling a kid at the auth module does NOT delete or disable the referencing SAs — the AlertServiceAccountsAffectedByKidBurn listener handles operator notification; the SAs remain queryable until an operator explicitly disables them or rotates to a new kid.

accessor_chain.description: ServiceAccount exposes computed accessors that downstream code reads instead of touching raw columns.

ServiceAccount.tenant_slug: joined from tenants.slug when tenant_id is populated; null otherwise
ServiceAccount.is_expired: expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < now()
ServiceAccount.is_expiring_soon: expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at BETWEEN now() AND now() + INTERVAL 'warning_days_before_expiry days'
